h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I was letting the copied words settle the meaning
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: address first, verb second, because the copied words alone cannot prove "turned back."
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was steadily testing whether 'turned back' could be justified by the evidence, moving toward either confirming or softening the verb.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the test narrow. The badge image can guide the caption, but the excerpt has to carry the claim. In the second excerpt, if the protester aims the copied line back at the chair's ruling and the room treats it as a reply, "turned back" fits. If the line is mainly for the crowd, "reused" or "echoed" is cleaner. Check one thing first: who is being answered at that moment?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: After L3 shows the learner drifting into the badge metaphor before checking the evidence, T3 strips the task to a single prior question (address before verb), shifting from open evidence-gathering to a sequenced, load-reducing diagnostic move.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot L4, so no recognition-contingent uptake can be scored.
